data.title: Here's What Is Pulling Apple Stock (AAPL) Higher Today, 7/24/2026
data.description:
Apple (AAPL) shares gained more than 3% on Friday after Ford (F) confirmed that Apple Maps will be built into its upcoming Universal Electric Vehicle Platform. This gives Apple a major boost in the EV software race. Analysts also added fuel to the rally. Morgan Stanley's Erik Woodring raised AAPL stock's price target to $364 from $360 and reiterated a Buy rating.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Ford's new EV platform is expected to support several advanced electric models. The company said Apple Maps will play a central role in navigation and charging guidance. For Apple, the integration strengthens its position in the auto sector without needing to build a car of its own.

Investors see the Ford partnership as another sign that Apple's services business is becoming a larger part of its long‑term growth story. Mapping and navigation tools are high-margin services, and deeper auto integration could help Apple grow its ecosystem and add new revenue as more people switch to EVs.

Analyst Says Apple Trades at a Rich Valuation

Morgan Stanley's Top analyst said Apple's core business is still very strong, but with the stock near record highs, the setup for earnings is tougher. Woodring said the company needs a clean report with no weak spots. The analyst thinks Apple can post solid revenue and EPS over the next year as it raises prices across many products, but notes the stock already trades at a rich valuation.

That means investors are pricing in strong results. However, any weakness, especially in Services growth or gross margin guidance, could weigh on the stock.

On the product side, Woodring said iPhone trends look healthy. Shipments jumped in the June quarter as Apple refilled channel inventory, and early July China data shows better demand. Supply chain checks point to steady iPhone production for 2026, though higher prices may slightly trim units.

Also, he noted Mac results are more mixed due to supply limits, but higher prices help offset lower shipments. Services growth should slow a bit because of weaker App Store trends, but recent Apple Music price hikes should help in the September quarter. Overall, Apple's long‑term outlook stays solid, but near‑term expectations are high, making the setup more neutral.

What Is the Price Target for AAPL?

Overall, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Apple stock. The analysts believe Apple's strong cash generation and high‑margin Services unit provide long‑term stability, even as rising memory costs and supply constraints create near‑term pressure.

The rating is based on 16 Buys, nine Holds, and two Sells recommendations. The average AAPL stock price target of $330.62 indicates 0.66% downside risk.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Burry's Bearish Bets Pay Off as July Selloff Hits Market Leaders
data.description:
Investor Michael Burry's latest bearish bets have mostly worked as several of the stocks he targeted fell sharply in July. Burry, who became famous for betting against the U.S. housing market before the 2008 financial crisis, said in a June 30 Substack post that he had shorted Tesla (TSLA), Caterpillar (CAT), Nvidia (NVDA), Applied Materials (AMAT), and the VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH). A short position makes money when a stock falls, so the recent pullback has moved four of those five trades in his favor.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

Explore NVDS for 2X short leverage on NVDA

Tesla has been Burry's strongest winner so far. He said he opened the short near $416.22, and the stock now trades at around $308 per share following disappointing quarterly results. That left Tesla down roughly 26% for July.

Caterpillar has moved sharply lower too, falling to around $894.54 from Burry's reported entry price of $1,060.98. The trade was unusual for him because he said Caterpillar had previously performed well for him as a long investment. Even so, the recent decline has made the short profitable on paper.

Semiconductor Bets Have Delivered a More Mixed Result

His semiconductor bets have delivered a more mixed result. The VanEck Semiconductor ETF dropped to roughly $556 from his disclosed short price of $642.80, while Applied Materials fell to about $541 from $729.40. Nvidia, however, moved against him by rising to around $208.76 from his reported entry near $198.09.

Overall, four of the five trades have gone in the direction Burry expected as investors pulled back from some of the market's strongest industrial and AI-related winners. However, it remains unclear whether he still holds any of these positions. Burry trades actively and often changes his portfolio quickly, so the June disclosure only shows where he entered the bets, not what he owns today.

Which Stock Is the Better Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, out of the four stocks mentioned above, analysts think that NVDA stock has the most room to run. In fact, NVDA's price target of $309.94 per share implies 48.5% upside potential. On the other hand, analysts expect the least from CAT, as its $1,016-per-share price target implies 14.2% upside. (See NVDA Stock Forecast).
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Will SpaceX Stock (SPCX) Drop to $100? What Would That Mean?
data.description:
Shares in aerospace and rocket firm SpaceX (SPCX) fell about 5% on Friday afternoon, have fallen about 18% over the past five days, and are now down about 50% from their June debut peak of $225.64. According to Morgan Stanley (MS), many investors expect SPCX stock could "realistically" hit $100 per share when the stock's first waves of lockup expiry hit. This would mean the market assigns zero to negative value for SpaceX's AI business.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

High conviction on SPCX? Try Tradr's SPCM or SPCG

Where Does $100/Share Leave SpaceX's Core Business?

On Friday, Morgan Stanley analyst Adam Jonas agreed that SpaceX shares could face further short-term pressure following the lockup expirations. CEO Elon Musk has said that early investors will be able to offload 911.5 million shares after the company releases its first-ever earnings as a public company on August 4.

The pressure could also come from earnings results that "may leave key investor questions unanswered," the analyst contended. If SPCX stock drops to $100 per share, investors would also be valuing SpaceX's core space and connectivity business significantly below Morgan Stanley's own estimates.

The analyst sees a "disconnect" between bearish sentiment about SpaceX's shares and the company's finances. The numbers have seen no major changes over the past few weeks, Jonas argued.

Why Morgan Stanley Remains Bullish on SpaceX Stock

Meanwhile, Jonas maintained his Buy rating on SPCX shares. He also assigned a price target of $300, predicting about 165% upside.

The Morgan Stanley analyst contended that the disconnect between bearish sentiment and SpaceX's unchanged financials creates "an attractive entry point" into the stock.

In addition, he argued that SpaceX remains "uniquely positioned" in its rocket launch, satellite connectivity, and AI business. It particularly boasts strong business advantages that protect future earnings for the first two.

What's more, Jonas believes the share price of less than $120 today is a "reasonable" value for SpaceX's earnings power. The current price also implies little to no value for SpaceX's opportunity in AI.

Who Is Adam Jonas?

Jonas is a four-star analyst who ranks in the top 17% of the more than 12,000 analysts tracked on TipRanks. He covers several companies in the auto and tech industries.

The analyst boasts a 52% success rate and has generated about 5.30% in average return for investors with his ratings.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Walmart on the Trail for More Vietnamese Suppliers in Global Expansion Push
data.description:
Retail giant Walmart (WMT) is looking to ramp up its Vietnamese supply chain.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The group has said it is joining the Vietnam International Sourcing 2026 conference in September in Ho Chi Minh City (HCMC). There it will be looking to expand its buying across clothes, footwear, home goods, furniture, processed foods and higher-value products.

Looking for Quality

"The key shift is quality. Walmart is not only looking for low-cost manufacturing. They need suppliers that can meet stricter standards around consistency, traceability, sustainability, product quality and delivery reliability," the company said in a LinkedIn post.

Although other companies have started hiking supplies from Vietnam as a result of increased Trump tariffs on China and concerns over the reliability of the supply, Walmart opened a sourcing office in HCMC in 2013, and has previously called Vietnam one of its most important sourcing markets in Southeast Asia.
In the first 6 months of 2026, Vietnam exported $86.5 billion of goods to the U.S., keeping America as Vietnam's largest export market.

"For Vietnamese suppliers, Walmart's return creates a bigger opening into one of the world's most powerful retail systems," Walmart said. "For US brands, the signal is just as important. Vietnam is becoming a more legitimate market to source from, sell into, and build around. When the world's biggest retailer keeps coming back, Vietnam is no longer just a "potential market". It is becoming part of the global retail map."

The event, jointly organized by Vietnam's Ministry of Industry and Trade and the Ho Chi Minh City administration, is meant to connect international buyers with Vietnamese suppliers and help develop the country's supply chain.

Vietnam in Demand

As part of its global sourcing diversification strategy, Walmart plans to supply the Vietnamese products through its network of more than 10,900 stores and clubs across 19 countries, serving approximately 280 million customers and members each week through physical stores and e-commerce platforms.

But businesses hoping to join Walmart's supply chain must demonstrate stable production capacity, consistent product quality, competitive pricing, and the ability to fulfill large-volume orders.

Last year, WMT said it was allowing its marketplace sellers to ship directly from Vietnam to the U.S. The U.S. retail giant said it was extending its cross-border ocean freight service beyond China by opening two Vietnamese ports to its marketplace sellers looking to ship full-container loads.

Is WMT a Good Stock to Buy Now?

On TipRanks, WMT has a Strong Buy consensus based on 25 Buy and 1 Hold ratings. Its highest price target is $155. WMT stock's consensus price target is $142.57, implying a 30.69% upside. (See WMT Stock Forecast).
data.author: David Craik

data.title: The Top 5 Biggest Game Stories This Week, 7/24/26
data.description:
The gaming world had a busy week thanks to game updates, business changes, new reveals, and other news. Here are the top five stories that stood out most.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

1. The Witcher 3 Expansion Gets a Big Reveal

CD Projekt (OTGLY) shared news on The Witcher 3. The new Songs of the Past expansion will get its first look at Gamescom Opening Night Live on August 25, 2026. This gives fans a date to look forward to for the latest update to The Witcher series.

2. Marvel Tōkon Open Beta Hits PS5 and PC

Sony's (SONY) Marvel Tōkon: Fighting Souls opened its beta test this week. Huge player demand caused some PlayStation Network issues. The game is set to be released on PS5 and PC on August 6, 2026.

3. Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced Beats Goals

Ubisoft (UBSFY) said Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced has done better than expected. The game passed its yearly sales goal in just two weeks. That marks a strong start for the title and could help pull UBSFY stock out of a recent rut.

4. Halo Makes Its Way to PlayStation

Microsoft's (MSFT) Halo: Campaign Evolved is coming to PlayStation, marking a first for the Halo series. The game will also receive a PC release, which has been the norm since the first game came out on PC back in 2003.

5. Xbox and EA Make Major Moves

Microsoft is testing a free cloud play tier for Xbox. The test lets players stream select games with ads. Microsoft also said old Xbox games are coming to PC, which is part of its effort to support retro games via backwards compatibility.

Electronic Arts (EA) also moved closer to a large deal. The EU approved a $55 billion bid for the company by the Public Investment Fund of Saudi Arabia. This is the same group that purchased SNK in 2022 and has investments in several other game studios.

Turning to the TipRanks stock comparison tool, traders can see how each of these gaming companies' stack up to each other. Investors can use this tool to gain further insight into the shares and weigh which ones might be good additions to their portfolios.
data.author: William White

data.title: U.S.-Iran Mediators Ramp Efforts to Revive Peace Talks as War Nears 5-Month Mark
data.description:
Pakistan is accelerating efforts to resume U.S.-Iran peace discussions as the war approaches its five-month mark, Pakistani sources told Reuters.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Earlier this week, Iranian Interior Minister Eskandar Momeni traveled to Islamabad to meet with top Pakistani officials. The group began preliminary talks to resume discussions, although the Pakistani sources cautioned that major hurdles remain. That comes as President Trump considers a major offensive campaign against Iran.

China Seeks Stability as U.S.-Iran Tensions Rise

China, the world's second-largest economy, also favors an end to the war. "The Chinese are unhappy because Iran's attacks on other Gulf states and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz are hitting their interests," said a Pakistani government official.

Tensions soared between the U.S. and Iran earlier this week after the Tehran-backed Houthi rebel group struck two Saudi tankers in the Bab al-Mandeb Strait. Trump warned that any future attacks on Saudi tankers would trigger severe consequences against both the Houthis and Iran.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Microsoft Stock (NASDAQ:MSFT) Notches Up on Plans to Support Open-Weight AI
data.description:
Tech giant Microsoft (MSFT) recently took a step forward in artificial intelligence (AI), backed up by a coalition of 25 tech firms who are all putting their weight behind a move to "…protect open-weight AI models from broad restrictions." The move was welcome enough, as far as investors were concerned. It did little to influence their interest in Microsoft stock, based on the fractional gain in share prices seen in Friday afternoon's trading.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

An "open-weight model," reports note, is basically AI with a lot of extra transparency included. Users can download open-weight models, look over them, make changes accordingly, and run them on local systems. Open-weight models give users access to high-end AI without the need to either build out their infrastructure to staggering degrees or pay frontier-model prices to do the jobs accordingly. Of course, the coalition also acknowledged security, noting that the defenders involved in cybersecurity need access to tools of the same class as their attackers.

The open letter the coalition issued in support of open-weight AI noted, "Our AI leadership will be judged not by one frontier AI model, but by whether the United States builds a strong, open ecosystem that diffuses into every sector."

Ad-Supported Tiers in Gaming

Meanwhile, Microsoft also rolled out a new notion in its Xbox division: free, ad-supported cloud gaming. The concept is hitting some Xbox Insiders first, and still comes with its share of caveats. Sessions are still limited to one hour per title, and will only be available for games you already own. Plus, you will have to watch the ad roll before the session begins, unless you have a "paid cloud gaming subscription."

Microsoft noted that cloud gaming could be helpful for Xbox One owners looking to play newer games without having to buy a new console. Though precisely how that is done when you can only play games that you already own is unclear. However, if Microsoft expands that out to offer up the entire library on cloud gaming, that could represent a significant leap forward. The role once played by game rentals—before the death of the video store as a concept—could be taken over by cloud gaming.

Is Microsoft a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on MSFT stock based on 36 Buys and one Hold assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 25.72% loss in its share price over the past year, the average MSFT price target of $555.73 per share implies 44.65% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Safety Insurance Group (SAFT) Stock Rockets on a $1.54B Deal
data.description:
Safety Insurance Group (SAFT) stock rocketed higher on Friday after the U.S.-based insurance company announced a merger agreement. The company agreed to be acquired by an affiliate of Mapfre S.A. (MPFRF) in an all-cash deal valued at about $1.54 billion. Under the agreement, Safety Insurance Group shareholders will receive $105 per share, a 44% premium to the company's stock price as of July 23, 2026.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The deal brings together two insurance companies with a focus on strong underwriting, customer service, and long-term growth. Safety Insurance Group will continue operating under its existing brand while gaining access to Mapfre's global scale, financial resources, and technology capabilities. The transaction is expected to close in the first quarter of 2027, pending regulatory approvals.

"This transaction represents an exceptional outcome for our shareholders and an exciting new chapter for Safety," said George Murphy, chairman and CEO of Safety Insurance Group. He added that the partnership will help the company invest in its people, improve its services, and expand its product offerings.

Investors will note that Safety Insurance Group's management team is expected to remain involved as the company transitions into a wholly owned unit of Mapfre S.A. This will see Safety Insurance Group become a sister company of Mapfre S.A.'s other companies.

Safety Insurance Group Stock Movement Today

Safety Insurance Group stock was up 41.35% on Friday, extending a 35.69% year-to-date gain. Investors will also note that the shares have risen 3.07% over the past 12 months.

SAFT stock trading activity today was heavy, as some 883,000 shares change hands. For the record, that's well above the company's three-month average daily trading volume of about 129,000 shares.

Is Safety Insurance Group Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, traditional analyst coverage of Safety Insurance Group is lacking. Fortunately, TipRanks' AI analyst Spark has it covered. Using the OpenAI – 5.2 model, Spark rates SAFT stock as Outperform with an $83 price target. It cites "strong underlying financial resilience" and "a highly favorable M&A catalyst with a large cash premium" as reasons for this stance. (See SAFT Stock Forecast)
data.author: William White

data.title: U.S. Business Activity Growth Accelerates on World Cup, July 4 Holiday
data.description:
U.S. business activity growth reached an eight-month high at the beginning of the third quarter, S&P Global (SPGI) said on Friday. The firm's U.S. Services Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) was 53.6 in July, topping expectations of 51.5 and up from 51.2 in June. A reading above 50 indicates expansion, while a reading below 50 points to contraction.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Within the index, services led the growth as the sector received a boost from the World Cup and the July 4 holiday. "Firms cited a lift from FIFA World Cup-related spending, stronger-than-usual July 4th activity and increased investment in sales, marketing and product development," said S&P Global.

Manufacturing Slips as Costs Continue to Grow

At the same time, manufacturing activity fell due to supply chain delays caused by the U.S.-Iran war. The Manufacturing PMI dropped to a four-month low of 53.8, declining from 53.9 in June. Manufacturers continued stockpiling inventory as a precaution amid price and supply concerns.

Meanwhile, S&P said input cost inflation hit its highest level since May 2025, driven by higher energy prices, shipping costs, and tariffs. In addition, selling price inflation approached a four-year high.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Tesla Stock (NASDAQ:TSLA) Slips as Tesla Reveals 2027 Models
data.description:
Interested in a vehicle from electric vehicle giant Tesla (TSLA)? Then you may want to wait a bit, and see what is coming out for 2027. It turns out that Tesla has a lot on tap for 2027, including some vehicles that will no longer be on hand. Investors did not seem happy about Tesla's plans for the new model year, and sent shares slipping nearly 3% in Friday afternoon's trading.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

High conviction TSLA bears now have this Tradr ETF

Reports note that the Cybertruck will likely remain untouched. There is an outside chance that Tesla will roll out new pricing, software, or equipment changes, but otherwise, the Cybertruck is likely to remain the same joyfully clunky, chunky wonder we all know and some of us love. The Model Y is also set to remain similar, because there was a big change for the 2026 model year. However, one big change is that the Model Y will now boast a complete five-seat seating array, after only having an optional third row, reports note.

The Model Y L is still set to arrive in 2027, complete with a three-row seating option. And yes, the Model X is still out of the picture as of 2027. A combination of mounting competition and an interest in streamlining operations pulled that one out of the picture, with 2027 being the first year no Model X will be made.

Check the Doors

One change, though, that could hit sooner than that—depending on the stance United States regulators ultimately take—is in Tesla's doors. A new rule-making process is apparently in place, designed after a set of incidents emerged in which drivers were trapped in their cars.

The new rules are aimed at cars whose door handles are flush with the vehicle, and operated electronically, like those found on Tesla cars. A petition called for new rules that require a mechanical door release that works in the event of an emergency, which honestly just makes sense. Rather than stage a defect investigation, the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) decided new rules were the route to go.

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 10 Buys, 15 Holds, and three Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 1.15% rally in its share price over the past year, the average TSLA price target of $386.65 per share implies 24.78% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson
